subject: Physics
title: Black Holes Warp Everything
description: A 60-second lesson on Black Holes Warp Everything.

hook.text: Near a black hole, gravity is so strong that not even light escapes once it crosses the edge.

buildup.text: Black holes form when massive stars collapse under their own gravity after burning all their fuel.

discovery.text: At the event horizon, time appears to freeze for an outside observer watching someone fall in.

twist.text: Supermassive black holes sit at the center of most galaxies—ours is four million solar masses.

climax.text: In 2019, the Event Horizon Telescope captured the first-ever photo of a black hole's shadow.

punchline.text: Gravity so strong, light gives up. The universe's final trap.

quiz.question: What is the event horizon of a black hole?
options: The boundary beyond which nothing can escape
The core where matter is crushed
The ring of light around it
